items: Live-scored 28 new candidate niches across 3 rounds (glycine, zinc carnosine, black garlic, tongkat ali, mucuna pruriens, magnesium bisglycinate, creatine and more) — ~22 strong finds (score >55) added to the hourly scan rotation.

⭐ Biggest find: a "Sleep & Calm Gummies" cluster — Melatonin Gummies (65.2), Magnesium Glycinate Gummies (63.1), Passionflower Gummies (60.5) and Glycine Capsules (64.4) all point to one flagship blend SKU that can out-quality 3.6★-4.0★ incumbents.
Discovered a format-gap pattern: L-Theanine & Lion's Mane Gummies are far less saturated than their capsule equivalents — full Why/How/Branding write-ups added (Rounds 3-6).
Hourly scan run #21 is testing the newly expanded ~530-niche seed list — first real-world check of this session's browser-reconnect fix.
🎨 Higgsfield reconnected — generating 4K packaging mockups for the top opportunities now.

✅ 3 new 4K packaging mockups are ready in Packaging Designs: Lymphatic Support Capsules, Aged Garlic Extract twin-launch, and 'Sleep & Calm Gummies' (the flagship blend SKU from the cross-round thesis).
📋 Full details (Why/How/Branding for every new opportunity) are in the Launch Plan.

🆕 Round 7: live-scored a Metabolic Health & Adaptogen cluster (10 candidates) — Berberine Gummies (62.5) has the lowest competition score of the whole session (57.1, just 69.5 avg reviews on top-10), and Rhodiola Rosea Capsules (61.4) found a comp with only 15 reviews/4.0★ but a "500+ bought/month" badge. Both added to the scan rotation — see Round 7 in the Launch Plan.

🆕 Round 8: Ceylon Cinnamon Capsules (60.9) confirms the Round 7 "Metabolic Health" cluster — a top comp combines Berberine + Ceylon Cinnamon + Chromium + Fenugreek in one product. Schisandra Berry (58.8) and White Mulberry Leaf Extract (56.7) also added to the scan rotation.

🔥 Big re-discovery: deeper research on the already-validated Cyperus Rotundus Oil opportunity (#3/#7/#13, score 72.3) found it has the highest accessible-listing ratio of the whole session — 11/22 sampled listings (50%) are low-review/high-velocity, including a listing with just 15 reviews and a "1000+ bought per month" badge. Every competitor uses generic stock photography — so we generated 5 brand-new premium 4K packaging concepts (Modern Apothecary, Spa Noir, Clean Clinical, Botanical Editorial, Soft Blush & Rose-Gold) to replace the original designs. See Packaging Designs.

🆕 Round 9 — major oversight found: re-audited all 536 scored opportunities and found "Pumpkin Seed Oil Softgels" sitting at #4 of 536 (opp 73.4) — higher than Aged Garlic Extract — and never written up. It opens a brand-new "Men's Health" category: a top comp shows "2000+ bought in past month" on just 26 reviews, and the related "Saw Palmetto Prostate Capsules" niche (#17) shows "600+ bought in past month" on 6 reviews/3.4★. Also surfaced Black Cohosh Capsules (#24, 65.4) — one of the lowest competition scores of the project, a candidate for a future "Women's Wellness" sub-brand.

🎨 First use of the real Earth & Essence logo in a Higgsfield generation — the gold serif wordmark + stylized ampersand was used as a reference image to launch a new forest-green & brass "Earth & Essence Men" sub-identity for the Pumpkin Seed Oil & Saw Palmetto Complex Softgels. See Packaging Designs.

🆕 Round 10 — direct line-extension found: "Bitters Liquid Drops" (#14 of 536) and "Digestive Bitters Tincture Drops" (#28) both lead to the exact same "Soursop Bitters" competitor cluster as Earth & Essence's own Soursop Liquid Extract — a near-zero-risk 3rd SKU for the existing liquid-drops line ("600+ bought in past month" on just 22 reviews). Also found "Collagen Lifting Body Oil" (#53) — a brand-new "2026" body-care trend with the lowest average competitor review count of the whole session (10.6), opening a potential new Body Care category for the brand. Full Why/How/Branding in the Launch Plan.

🆕 Round 11 — triple-confirmation pass: two more independent search terms reconfirm Round 10's finds — "advanced collagen oil" (#74) shares a competitor ASIN directly with the Collagen Lifting Body Oil cluster (#53), and "sea moss liquid drops" (#105) shares three competitor ASINs with the Soursop Bitters Drops cluster (#14/#28) — both now confirmed from a 3rd angle. Also found "Chasteberry (Vitex) Capsules" (#139) with a 42.1 competition score — the lowest of any real-demand opportunity in the whole 536-niche dataset, a natural dual-formula companion to Round 9's Black Cohosh "Women's Wellness" line. Plus a brief mention of "Pygeum Bark Extract" (#98) as a potential 3rd ingredient for the "Earth & Essence Men" Pumpkin Seed Oil & Saw Palmetto Complex. Full Why/How/Branding in the Launch Plan.

images.label: Pumpkin Seed Oil & Saw Palmetto — "Earth & Essence Men"

items: 🆕 First dedicated opportunity audit for Pure Pleasure — 115 niches scored so far, with a deeper re-scan running now to find more (previous two scan attempts crashed mid-run; this one is in progress).

⭐ Biggest find: Anal Beads (Silicone) — opportunity 65.2, but the standout number is competition: just 49.4, the lowest of any real-demand niche in the category, with top listings averaging only 19.6 reviews against ~10,000 search results. This is the cleanest low-moat opportunity found in Pure Pleasure so far.

🆕 Nipple Clamps (64.6 opp, 56.2 competition, 52.1 avg reviews) and Remote Control Cock Ring (64.4 opp, 54.3 competition, just 39.3 avg reviews) both open brand-new sub-categories — neither vibrators nor lubricants, so zero overlap with the existing PurePleasure Wand/Rabbit Vibrator lines.

💡 Listing-optimisation lead: "Silicone G-Spot Wand" scores highest overall (69.5 opp, 63.5 competition) and may be a near-zero-cost win for the existing PurePleasure Wand Vibrator — same logic as the Lymphoria find for Earth & Essence: add "g spot" targeting to the current listing rather than building a new SKU.

⚠️ Avoid the lubricant trap: Water-Based Lubricant, Anal Lubricant Gel and Personal Lubricant all score 67-69 on opportunity, but competition sits at 97.5 with top listings averaging 6,000-30,000+ reviews — among the most saturated searches tracked anywhere in this project. High demand alone doesn't make these viable; do not prioritise.
Anal Training Kit (63.8 opp, 71.4 avg reviews) and Pocket Pussy Stroker (64.1 opp, men's solo category) round out the next tier — see Packaging Designs for the live ranked list.
